author: ZiggyMUD
content:
@SW4GN3R 
Why doesn't ATMTA air drop an item (similar to the Genesis Token that Saga phone owners get) for all Faction Fleet owners over a certain rank? This token would be non transferable and non tradable. This item would be tied to the player's wallet of their choosing. The beauty of the Genesis Token idea is that anyone could reward these early supporters of Star Atlas Not just ATMTA. Maybe companies that use the Star Atlas IP could offer special deals, the DAO could do a cool giveaway, maybe a future hard copy of CORE could be randomly given away, maybe a future Space Station, freight service, rescue repair/fuel service in Star Atlas may want to give a small discount to the players that made Star Atlas possible. Maybe large content creators could do a random ship giveaway to these players. If you're interested in the idea connect up with Solana Mobile as they might be able to help.

This token would be non transferable and non tradable. This item would be tied to the player's wallet of their choosing. The beauty of the Genesis Token idea is that anyone could reward these early supporters of Star Atlas Not just ATMTA. Maybe companies that use the Star Atlas IP could offer special deals, the DAO could do a cool giveaway, maybe a future hard copy of CORE could be randomly given away, maybe a future Space Station, freight service, rescue repair/fuel service in Star Atlas may want to give a small discount to the players that made Star Atlas possible. Maybe large content creators could do a random ship giveaway to these players. If you're interested in the idea connect up with Solana Mobile as they might be able to help.

content: So here

author: Signal
content: Technically this is a question, and it’s being asked in FR.  I love the vibe, the interior the look the feel the whole aesthetic it’s awesome and totally me (like this whole hippie peace warrior thing).  I love tanks in games, and this thing is as tanky as it gets.  Bombers remind me of cave trolls in Lord of the Rings.  Big, crazy tough, brutes, that just get punched in the face until they get into range and then just demolish stuff.  I philosophically can sympathize with the ECOS and their quest to undo what mankind did to its own planet, and their thirst to return to a more natural, simple world.  Take all of that, and it can haul cargo when the fighting isn’t needed and it becomes this awesome armored behemoth of a wagon cart.  What’s not to love?

author: odvb
content: The purpose of the Council is to curate proposals and work with proposal creators to ensure they conform to guidelines, which are being defined right now by community participation in PIP-1. The Council therefore cannot precede the definitions of that framework, so in a way we are bypassing that step. But I do want to emphasize that no one is voting for PIP-1 right now. Community Interest is a mechanism that will allow the community to bring to the surface those proposals that are most important to them. That way they can be prioritized for debate/discussion and ultimately making their way through to a floor vote.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Quick note here - from a collector's standpoint, yes a 1/100 object is good, but less so if the primary sale price attempts to bake in the "collectible value". Undercutting the price-relative economic productivity of early releases is what is worrying a lot of people lately. I am sure you are aware of this discussion. And if primary sales continues to undercut previous primary sales (and therefore directly and immediately harming secondary markets), it creates insecurity to buy anything. I think most early buyers of ships were expecting similar economic value + collector value on top of that. So $300 1/100 early purchase ship should be similar productivity as $300 1/1000 release ship, not a $60 1/1000 ship.   We are still waiting for some more official comments on this, e.g. the Lowbie-Jetjet value proposition.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: I've been doing a bit of information gathering on this myself, as I was not deeply involved in the v2 config effort. While I do want to fully refine a response, what I can provide right now is that a number of variables were factored into the decision making process:  Establishing better balance across classes / assets was a primary objective (doesn't address price disparity), Initial ship rarity + crafting difficulty was considered in the pricing model, primarily through manufacturer premiums and discounts, which still applies to all future sales with the same adjustment values applied. Similar to the discussion you and I were having in FR, while this might not be fully appreciated today, it is a function that we assume will have an impact in the future, also assuming substantial growth in the user base. In particular, the cost to acquire rarer materials alongside the risks that must be absorbed to execute missions in HRZ directly contribute to future availability of models that have been priced at a premium. It is an estimate of the future supply/demand equation. So the question is, if ships have similar performance, will certain models still be in demand? Which I'll address in points 3 & 4., As you know, each of these ships has a unique and novel design. Including difficult to quantify traits like visibility out of a cockpit, interior elements, as well as overall visual appeal of some manufacturers over others. By way of analogy, we could say that both a 1995 Honda and a 2023 Mercedes can get you from point A to point B. But Mercedes still sells cars, at a premium, because there are willing and able buyers to purchase those vehicles for a variety of reasons. We believe the same will apply to ship models. Star Atlas is not catering exclusively to the spreadsheet gamer who cares only about base metrics, but emphasizes the visual fidelity of all assets in the game., Through the release of v2 config information, we made every attempt to articulate that you are not receiving full details on the new configs. Notably, the ship interiors / modules are not included in the current information set, as they will be refined as the ships are actually modeled in UE. More context will also follow as to intrinsic benefits of each manufacturer., Dampening the impact of previous ship pricing relative to the updated model, all of the current ships have been able to produce value based on VWAP (not price paid) through Faction Fleet since 2021. And while it's true that not all ships have been enlisted since that date, it's also true that most ships have been trading below VWAP since April of 2022, and therefore were receiving greater levels of income relative to purchase price than the original model., We did run an analysis of previous ship prices against the new model to determine variance, and almost all ships fall within a +/- 5-15% range. There are a few outliers, namely the Lowbie/JetJet, and the Unabomba, which might require some further adjustment, but that needs to be discussed in more detail with Game Design. However, those were always unique cases. The Lowbie was priced at $60 when all other XS ships were priced $120+, and Unabomba was priced at $35 when XXS were priced $15-$20. So while the new model exacerbates that difference, it was always intrinsic to those specific ships., I can't directly speak to the implications of ship descriptions and function relative to new descriptions. That is best handled by Danny (not tagging as he is out of office for some personal family matters), or by @Deleted User / @Sean Lynn. I'm simply providing some context on the economic components.,

author: Schrodinger_brMUD
content:
I wrote this note on Thursday morning that was well received by community members. I'd like to replicate this message again so it doesn't get lost in conversations and reaches as many members as possible.
Ship specification update notes:
1 – Ships were leveled regardless of their price, causing distortion in the market and being unfair to the players who paid more to own better ships.
2 – The Orgika Mik that costs U$ 710 is apparently better in terms of combat than the Pearce X6 that costs U$ 930.
3 – The VZUS Opod is described as a “stealthy” ship when in fact it has the worst heat sink of all manufacturers.
4 - The Pearce F4, which is an expensive ship, was excellent. Now it has been “equalized” to ships that are cheaper. The differential of “beating above the category”, with the multiplication factor 2.71, simply ceased to exist.
5 – The Pearce C9 that previously had a firepower that was compatible with the MUD faction advantage (highest class firepower) has now been leveled down along with the rest.
6 – From what I observed, there is almost no difference in firepower between the ships. The firepower of the MUDs on the ships, which is one of the main characteristics of the faction (firepower and travel distance), was not taken into account. Only an extra point by the manufacturer is ridiculous compared to a reinforced hull of a higher category than the class that some ships have. Also, Pearce ships have the worst maneuverability in combat and should at least have greater firepower to compensate this disadvantage, thus respecting the particularity of the faction.

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content: Using the lore.staratlas info, the only difference I saw between the Mik and the X6 is agility, which the latter lacks, and jump range, which is reduced on the former. Damage-wise, they're equal (again, assuming the information found on the lore page).  I'd still say the X6 is superior, as you have more weapons to fire from. Less damage output per damage but higher fire density. Assuming you can swap missile bays with something else, you have more hardpoints to work with. This reasoning is an unlikely scenario, though.  Even so, and I'm grossly oversimplifying for the sake of keeping this short, due to the smaller weapons, you probably have lower running costs too. There are advantages in this, since the mats you need to craft your ammo/missiles are less as well.

author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content: Usually you’d want to fly first person if you are into dogfighting. You would just want nothing blocking your FOV.  The more I mess around in the dogfighting module the more I stick with single seat ships. I love the X6 but the Ambwe has an amazing 1st person cockpit. It will eventually be obstructed…but it’s still single seater.  Our meta could be different. People could fly 3rd person. But if we end up like most games it will be 1st person.   For all we know there will be several meta dogfighters—but like SC, the ones that are meta (Gladius and Arrow) are single seaters.  It will be fun to find out how all this takes shape later on in UE5

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: I agree. It's no different that what happens in the military. You go for R&D, then prototype building, field testing, refining, then create assembly lines and the logistics to support mass production. Oftentimes, you plan 30 ships but only 5 get delivered and you spent 5bn more than originally anticipated, with an overrun of ~2 years.  It happens. And, translating this to SA, given that we've been in this game for a couple of years, I think most of us know any date given is a tentative date and not a definite one.  But like someone in the FR was asking, when I inquired concerning REC deployment was to get a very rough estimate of how much R4 I'd require to have a "runway". Without SAGE to earn, or the DAO to sell, we must purchase our R4 from P2P transactions via the marketplace, or acquire additional claim stakes/mining ships.  The only change here is that the DAO isn't selling anymore. But it was enough to make both options less viable due to the high long-term cost of the former and the high initial investment for the latter.  It'd be nice to have a sunset with SAGE and a sunrise with REC but this is not the case. So, some fleets are likely to run out of R4 until then.

author: [EC] mad.Arbaf [STAR]
content: Cargo and fuel will always be intrinsic to your ship. I'm not sure I fully understand the question. Your inventory will be comprised of separate slots. Separate ones for some of the consumable resources like food, fuel, and ammunition. Those reserves are utilized to operate your ship. You'll have a separate cargo hold that can also hold a variety of resources, including those R4. However, those R4 are not immediately consumable for operations - they would need to be transferred to the "tanks" that use them. It is analagous to having fuel in the fuel tank of your car, and also having a fuel cannister in your trunk.

reply.preview: Cargo and fuel will always be intrinsic to your ship. I'm not sure I fully understand the question. Your inventory will be comprised of separate slots. Separate ones for some of the consumable resources like food, fuel, and ammunition. Those reserves are utilized to operate your ship. You'll have a separate cargo hold that can also hold a variety of resources, including those R4. content: not sure. I think that was mentioned (best warp in fleet), but IMO that makes no sense - you could throw in an x4 into any fleet and get near max warp. It should either be mininum of your fleet (makes sense to me) or a weighted average based on ship class or fleet points maybe

reply.preview: not sure. I think that was mentioned (best warp in fleet), but IMO that makes no sense - you could throw in an x4 into any fleet and get near max warp. It should either be mininum of your fleet (makes sense to me) or a weighted average based on ship class or fleet points maybe

author: defiantwyvern
content: to me, it depends on, conceptually, if the ships are individually warping, or combining warp power. If they individually warp, it makes sense to only warp to minimum distance. If the fleet can combine warp powers - it would make sense to combine the class-adjusted total warp power, then divide by the class-adjusted total "mass"

author: Xcode [STAR]
content: @Xcode   The topic of DACs and Guilds has come up again. During internal discussions, several Star Atlas oriented business have posed whether or not the DAC platform will have sufficient ability to differentiate to the public between Gameplay focused DACs and Business focused DACs (not that either are mutually exclusive) - but the concern exists amongst both ATMTA and the general public on how we best handle the desired neutrality of Business DACs.   Would it be possible to do an AMA or have a small group discussion on the matter?

During internal discussions, several Star Atlas oriented business have posed whether or not the DAC platform will have sufficient ability to differentiate to the public between Gameplay focused DACs and Business focused DACs (not that either are mutually exclusive) - but the concern exists amongst both ATMTA and the general public on how we best handle the desired neutrality of Business DACs. Would it be possible to do an AMA or have a small group discussion on the matter?

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: Speed: Warp is faster but consumes more fuel. Not ready to share relative speeds for now.,Kind of how EV works right now. What we will aim to decrease is the warp speed, so we'll gradually adjust the pace of the game to what it'll look like for the full game.,It'll average everything except max_warp_distance. We aim to use sci-fi assumptions here and through Alcubierre's Drive, smaller ships can "come along" for the ride within the field created by the largest warp drive. The other exception is subwarp_speed since it'll take the slowest within the fleet.,

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: Maybe once the DAC platform MVP comes out we can talk about the future of the platform and how we imagine our users making use of it (both businesses and gaming guilds).   The MVP focuses on; registration,management and permissions,searching and applying to a DAC, This is to help DACs establish themselves on chain and get organized.   Businesses can remain "Unaligned"  which will set them apart from in game DACs, but will prevent them from actually playing.  (They can later create faction aligned sub DACs to play in game if they wish).

content: not sure how complex y'all will want it to be when the time comes, but since components will be swappable objects, it makes sense to me to have the performance bonuses tied to components. Hull could keep some portion of the current make bonus, and components could have the other portion.

author: defiantwyvern
content:
@ROME | KING SHRED The component slot size is just a way to make a "big number" difference between the manufacturers, one that has a fixed upper limit compared to other manufacturers. I'd rather not see all makes of ships running around maxed out with equal stats. Having some notable differences between the manufacturers in end-game level ships seems good.

Also ... what if the components all remained in-class, but they increased the impact of the make bonuses a lot? At the end of the day, they are going to get crunched down to numbers. It almost doesn't matter as long as the numbers come out the same. Other than whether or not things make "sense". But looking at the various different ship designs, moving some components from one ship to another probably wouldn't be practical anyway. Even if they were the same class.

author: defiantwyvern
content:
I'd probably prefer any component size differences be configured on a per-ship basis, instead of across manufacturers. Especially for manufacturers that make ships across a wide variety of roles. Some ships currently feel a bit off, because of that. But seems like the V2 spec was a way for them to clean up balance across all ships in a pretty straightforward way, instead of worrying about dozens of ships all with unique qualities.

At least they have lots of input from the community now.  maybe too much hah

An engaging concept that links persons into the SA Metaverse through an external activity without necessitating a ship.  New players can begin participating through the crew markets and earn themselves a startup ship.
Using a capped character progression system is excellent since some people will look for ways to exploit the process. 

Overall, I like this initial implementation and the ideas for expansion/integration into the SA ecosystem.

author: ROME | KING SHRED [STAR]
content: @ROME | KING SHRED The component slot size is just a way to make a "big number" difference between the manufacturers, one that has a fixed upper limit compared to other manufacturers. I'd rather not see all makes of ships running around maxed out with equal stats. Having some notable differences between the manufacturers in end-game level ships seems good.  Also ... what if the components all remained in-class, but they increased the impact of the make bonuses a lot? At the end of the day, they are going to get crunched down to numbers. It almost doesn't matter as long as the numbers come out the same. Other than whether or not things make "sense". But looking at the various different ship designs, moving some components from one ship to another probably wouldn't be practical anyway. Even if they were the same class.

author: Odicerous
content:
Agreed.  I am not a fan of the C9 revisions either.  The original was all business.  Hard edged, clunky, rough, military looks.   Focus on function, stylish lines doesn't win fights.  That is the bad ass ship I wanted. The new version is more polished, prettier, and doesn't seem nearly as reconfigurable.
Does it still look good? Yes.
Is it still a formidable ship? I'm sure.
Do I trust the design team? Of course.
But I like the old version better.  It had more Pearce brutishness.

author: PG I BULK
content:
This a message for all the players.

What happened is super normal to happen in all companies, already happened to me in 2009, and then the company recover. Also happen on the other side, I was hired because the company recover from a bad moment.

What they did was correct and the best decision, I'm sure that the majority of the employees already were waiting for this situation after the FTX situation. So is not a surprise to no one.

SA has 2 products SAGE and UE5, the team decide to focus on the game that is closer to release to the masses, the UE5 was always going to take more than 5 years, so if they focused on the browser version of the game could be released this year is a smart strategy.

FYI: The browser game will bring utility to the NFTs.

I have a good investment in this game, and I'm not worried at all at the moment.

But again, I'm only responsible for myself, and everyone is responsible for each of you.

Thanks.
PG

author: Lorddo [ALRT]
content:
I agree that ue5 was their unique selling point amongst crypto game competitors, amongst with one of the first big games in ue5, which means the most expensive developers and trying to develop on that + new tech like crypto might be a too big of a step all at once.
So pivoting to Sage could be your only way to save the project. Because with time and expect AI to greatly improve this process it might be way easier to develop that part, allowing you to catch up. I do hope that UE5 gets some gameplay, even if it's just a racing game or something. I think fleshing out a part that you can sell as a separate game your able to start attracting your real gamers already, have leagues and events, leaderboard that work and only focus on that part in UE5 till it's a full game, then pick a next part and create that into a separate game, your metaverse will come when you have enough games to combine, it's a longer route and I hope that you guys also willing to go community games, where they can actually use your models and have cool ideas made with that. The IP is already there so it's just one more step now you guys skin down, why not scale up the community?
Coex shows that they are capable of a lot with little, imagine what they can do with a kick start like that. Utility brings people, people bring cash, doesn't matter from who this utility comes, hell I think for SEC it might even be better if first utility comes from others

I know we will pull through because we are with many. Live happens and sometimes things get in your way that change the entire structure like we see happening, if we stay strong together I am sure we come out kicking on the other end

author: MrTixlUstur
content: Here is a head scratcher…when Star Atlas ran it’s load test, there was a period of time this project was over 10% of the Solana daily transactions. author: Odicerous
content:
Thank you for the Meeting today.  The plan sounds great.Expanding the player base is the future of SA.  But please consider player retention as equally important.  It makes no sense to bring in new players until the game is functional and polished enough to retain them.
I think the steps should be: 

Launch Sage Labs for the existing user base to explore, break, and fine tune.  We are used to that and excited to test it.,
Produce a great UI. Not a good one.  It  MUST be GREAT.  Intuitive and visually exciting.,While we don't want to chase the same user group as Heist, the success of their clean and "fun" UI can't be ignored.  Its not even a game, but people think of it as one because of the UI.The lesson to learn is that an intuitive and polished interface is essential for rapid adoption of a wide variety of players.

Once there is:,a functional and entertaining game-a clean, efficient, and attractive UI.,and an easy way to onboard,THEN launch the mobile app, crew training, referral programs, ground racing expansion, and other modules to grow the user base.
The numbers will grow exponentially.  The hype will be yuuge and the game play will support it.You are trying to build momentum... you can't afford to have the next wave of new players get bored, frustrated, and quit because the game is not quite "there".

Make SAGE great, and we will take it from there.

My guess would be that the sooner Sage is successful, the sooner the DAO gets more love from the devs, but for now, it just isn't a critical next step.

author: Chri.z [STAR]
content: Got the go ahead to drop some additional alpha on ATLAS sinks in SAGE. Currently, you'll have to have a small amount (and I mean a teeny tiny amount) of SOL in your wallet in order to cover Signage fees for the solana bc. We are planning on incorporating an ATLAS pair so that players can sign these fees in ATLAS without the need to have any SOL in their wallet whatsoever. This is advantageous for a number of reasons but one of the biggest perk is that it provides additional sinks and utility for ATLAS. I for one am pumped about this feature.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content:
Mobile pros: faster rollout, more users = more hype on future ui updates, adds value to showroom, and crew/ships (by association), adds unique utility, generates revenue short term (from crew sale market fees), gives community devs time to build their own apps, adding value while also potentially influencing SA to add certain features. Developing an Leads to mobile compatibility to sage long term. Users will go out walking and taking about SA. 

cons for mobile: new users are met with labs instead of rec, we have to play a more rudimentary game in the meantime. Nasty memes and content about SA promising ue5 and rolling out a mobile walking app. Its an auxiliary release, and not perfectly in line with our main goals, and might even delay future releases. 

Pros for UI: we get a cleaner game faster, we can start creating good looking content on social media. We focus on the main line of development rather than an auxiliary application. 

Cons for UI: We don’t have as many users to share it with, takes longer, there’s, no added utility (just better UI) doesn’t directly benefit the showroom. Gives time to adjust levers in labs.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content:
I see mobile app as a parallel dev project. I see the great list @[AEP] Bodhitree compiled. However I strongly believe that focus should be 100% on SAGE. This means Sage Labs > Sage REC(UI) > Sage Combat. 

The mobile can be developed in parallel at its own pace (slow) and released when it is ready. Getting audience to see SAGE and play with it is critical to maintain the momentum. 

Mobile app may bring in new users but it will be a distraction with very little conversion to actual game. Lets face it. We will not have 1M users on day one. While that user base builds up it will create all sort of PR and support issues that will take time and effort to manage. Not worth it. 

Build 3 Stage iterations that we know about and get them out this year. If not we wont see Sage till Q1 2024 at least. That will not be good.

Build 3 Stage iterations that we know about and get them out this year. If not we wont see Sage till Q1 2024 at least. That will not be good.

author: Odicerous
content: Mobile pros: faster rollout, more users = more hype on future ui updates, adds value to showroom, and crew/ships (by association), adds unique utility, generates revenue short term (from crew sale market fees), gives community devs time to build their own apps, adding value while also potentially influencing SA to add certain features. Developing an Leads to mobile compatibility to sage long term. Users will go out walking and taking about SA.   cons for mobile: new users are met with labs instead of rec, we have to play a more rudimentary game in the meantime. Nasty memes and content about SA promising ue5 and rolling out a mobile walking app. Its an auxiliary release, and not perfectly in line with our main goals, and might even delay future releases.   Pros for UI: we get a cleaner game faster, we can start creating good looking content on social media. We focus on the main line of development rather than an auxiliary application.   Cons for UI: We don’t have as many users to share it with, takes longer, there’s, no added utility (just better UI) doesn’t directly benefit the showroom. Gives time to adjust levers in labs.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: My idea is for this to be a temporary measure because let's face it, things aren't looking good overall. NFT's are having the worst time since inception.  From my interaction with nft enthusiasts across multiple projects, I see that they want utility for their NFTs, and we have (an upcoming) game. That can earn them some money. There aren't many people left in the space and we could sure use their support here, it would make the game more fun .with more players, it would drive demand for assets, and it would certainly help other people join.  That's why the heist is so popular right now because the yield is worth it. The burn/lock of atlas tokens would help drive the price higher and increase yield. The tokens aren't actually gone, they will be reintroduced again (at a pace that won't mess up the economy).  Will all the new players stay? Who knows, people get bored eventually of anything. But if they earn a decent amount, they will certainly shill it to other people and the user base would grow. High atlas price means ATMTA can sell their tokens (or even better -- new assets -- and there will be people to buy them) and they can return back to UE5 building. Actually everyone wins in this scenario.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: You cannot increase yields arbitrarily. Another way to do the same thing would be to just give ships score x% more atlas. I’ll give the following example to illustrate my point and show how the value would flow in the case of burning tokens. For simplicity sake let’s say there is 1k tokens worth $1 each and SA owns 500 of them, the other 500 are owned by 100 people who own 5 each. It’s as close to the actual distribution as I can get while still making the example simple. Let’s say SA burns %10 of the supply (100 tokens) and this raises the price per token %10 consequentially (would certainly happen) now each of the 100 people with 5 each just made %10 going from $5 to $5.5. Since there is no actual utility when it comes to burning itself. Those 100 players would be wise to sell %10 of those tokens since they just made that money from nowhere. Meaning there was no utility involved in raising the price. If the increase had been due to some added utility that added %10 value then they should hold because the value would accurately reflect the actual value of the project as a whole. But because the value arbitrarily went up (lowered supply arbitrarily raises the price unlike utility)  it would be wise for the original 100 players to take their free %10 windfall. Sadly this would come from those who should have showed up because of the %10 pump news

author: [AEP] Siphaawal
content: Hi Team, @SW4GN3R , this is in relation to the companion mobile app Vs SAGE priority. I feel we are over estimating how many people will take the racing career. Currently we have around 5-6k active players in our community. If I make a liberal guess we will have a maximum of 1k people who will be racing or pursue the racing career. Most of them will be training their crew themselves and may buy a few or no crew members. I agree there may be a initial spike of people thinking they will be able to sell the crew member to a Star Atlas racer but due the my guessed demand that spike will quickly die down and most of those people will leave. We may benefit for the initial spike but that will not last long. However, SAGE economy is why most of us are here. Helping test that with SAGE Labs ( which will also create work for the teams to fix bugs the community find) will solidify the on chain logic for UE5 and later SAGE releases. I believe to have an influx of player we need player progression, profile, loaning assets to "scholars" and a fully functional economy ( with combat ) with a pleasant/polished UI of SAGE. We are so close! Please use the next six months to finish a good portion of SAGE.

author: [AEP] Siphaawal
content: If this app is an absolute success, the crew will cost pennies because of supply vs demand and will quickly cease to be a "Move To Earn". If it fails, they will only have wasted resources. I think they should stop wasting resources or pivot the app's mechanics to target gamers who enjoy space games. What worries me the most is that we need to come here and expose the obvious...that Star Atlas' target audience is space gamers. The people who just wanted to make money through the failed "P2E" model have already left.

author: 13omb3r
content: Token burning is kind of like 'share buybacks' for public company stocks. It may increase the share price of the publicly traded stock but it doesn't really do anything to drive productive value into the company. If anything, I would argue that it is a poor use of funds for a company to buy back its stock instead of investing in productive assets that would generate value for the company. Now burning tokens doesn't require cash from the team, but it is kind of meaningless I think in terms of actually adding productive value to the ecosystem in order to generative a higher eventual value in the ecosystem that should then drive the Polis value higher. And burning sets a bad precedent if it doesn't drive the intended results. You burn 10% today, it only generates 5% of 'price upside'. So do you have to burn 20% tomorrow to generate 10% upside? It tends to be a declining return profile

author: [HEIM] Drakkainen
content: Burning tokens increases trust, which increases the possibility of bullish. Assuming they burn 20%, if the token appreciates 50%, the remaining 80% will be worth more than the initial 100%. And they can start with a smaller burn to test market response, 10%, for example. Even meme coins benefit from burning tokens, I imagine a serious project like Star Atlas will benefit even more.

author: BRUNN
content: That might be a little optimistic… Eve Online only has between 15k and 20k users on at any one time. It is one of the most successful multiplayer space games. 1 million humans might be a bit of a stretch givin the crypto barriers to entry. If we had a DAU greater then 30k we would be one of the most popular MMOs around. Looking at the steam charts for lost ark, gw2, and new world will show some of idea or relative lv if you account for clientside logins as well being about the same. 1million MAU which i think is the number your looking at, in one year would be a crazy success… and would put us on the same lv as genshin impact or pokemon go.

author: BRUNN
content: Axie peaked at 3M, but it was in the mad fever of the novelty of NFT games. At that time, we believed that we could earn a lot of money from these games/. Which was true at first, but quickly collapsed, like all pyramid schemes.

author: AndrewB [GW]
content: Also all these games mention are fun and engaging games… so the people playing them are gamers… who will stick around after the hype if they like to game. The people playing axie and any other crypto game are investors… and as soon as the earing potential dries up they will hit the road because it doesnt make economic sense to stay. For every crypto game there is a better and more fun web2 game… so it will be very hard to win over gamers with the fun factor… which is what they need to stay. Cause when the fun dries up they will just go back to stellaris, eve, star citizen, the upcoming starfield… etc

author: ZiggyMUD
content:
About 50,700 POLIS tokens a day are being released to those staking POLIS. Or an 8.3% increase in annual POLIS in circulation.

Currently about 33% of this daily reward goes to ATMTA which Wagner has stated will be sold at a reduced rate to produce consistent cash flow.,The remaining 67% of the daily rewards will go to POLIS stakers, of which 85% or so are locked for 5 years. This will likely mean many if not most will not be willing to sell once good gameplay comes out, reaffirming their long term vision for Star Atlas.,
After the vesting token distributions finally completes in September the only way new POLIS tokens will enter the market will be through those staking in the POLIS locker (where the longer locked players will receive a larger % of daily reward pool) & in-game rewards. Both distribution methods will likely be to players more dedicated to the Star Atlas vision.

Could we see a possibility where from now until the top of the next bull run the POLIS liquid circulating supply might only grow by ATMTAs daily 16,400 staking rewards on top of a 37,500,000 base?
16,400*365= ~6,000,000 POLIS Annual ATMTA staking rewards

Definitely not financial advice.
Just my own opinion, but please debunk anything above.

Again, I would be interested to hear your thoughts?

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: @Darth Venkman I completely understand what you’re saying but we need solid standards for pricing assets as well as conveying value to customers. People need to make educated purchases of these assets. The fact that there are arbitrary designations on a whole range of assets makes it extremely difficult for people who have been around the project to judge an asset. Now let’s hopefully get mass adoption. This little issue of unclear/inconsistent valuation of assets(please note I’m not saying that they are valued improperly I’m saying we can’t see how they are valued differently or what causes the difference in valuation) creates a huge problem. Now let’s offer the same assets for purchase without crew components etc. everything becomes extremely hard to value. If it’s too much of a bother for the team allow them to resource it. Just give us the info and the community can help display this info for consumers to make educated decisions and not have a bad taste in their mouth when they thought they bought something and it turned out to be different
